old_claim.note: Leather Working provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct leather_working edge remains because the source-backed launch-quality evidence is a ceramic pot-bellows and tuyere-nozzle assemblage, not a documented leather bag or hide membrane.

source_shape.source_locator: Area A Stratum A2 table and Iron Age I remains narrative list pot-bellows and tuyere nozzles with metal-working remains, without identifying leather working as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The excavation report supports the bellows node through ceramic pot-bellows evidence and does not document a leather-working dependency for that evidence.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping a leather_working edge would require a separate source for hide or leather bellows construction; the launch-quality source only pins preserved pot and tuyere components.

ontology_before: Bellows depended on leather_working through expert inference, but the edge had no source tied to the cited first-known bellows evidence.
ontology_after: Bellows keeps only the sourced pottery path for the Iron Age pot-bellows assemblage and does not imply a direct leather-working prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: bellows no longer directly depends on leather_working.
would_reject_if: A source for the same early bellows evidence explicitly identifies a preserved leather or hide bellows component.
The node is narrowed to leather bag bellows and no longer uses the Abel Beth Maacah pot-bellows chronology.
A separate leather_bag_bellows node is added and should carry a leather_working prerequisite.
short_reason: The sourced first-known evidence documents pot bellows, not leather-working practice.
